hierarchy.name: Intersect MBO — Who Holds Power
hierarchy.description: Intersect is a Wyoming nonprofit corporation (incorporated July 13, 2023) created by IOG, EMURGO, and the Cardano Foundation. The board of directors manages all corporate affairs per the bylaws. Committees are advisory — they provide recommendations but cannot make binding decisions. This chart shows documented authority, not marketing language.

power_levels.governing: Can make binding decisions. Has legal authority over corporate affairs.
power_levels.executive: Manages day-to-day operations. Appointed by governing body.
power_levels.advisory: Provides recommendations and proposals. Cannot make binding decisions. Cannot approve spending, hire/fire, or veto board decisions.
power_levels.elected_voice: Can elect representatives. Cannot directly approve budgets, contracts, or operational decisions.

children.name: Board of Directors
children.subtitle: Governing Body — Binding Authority
children.details: The board manages all affairs of the corporation per the Intersect bylaws (governed by Wyoming Nonprofit Corporation Act). The board appoints the Executive Director, sets strategic direction, authorizes contracts, and sets member dues. Board decisions are not subject to member vote. No public board meeting minutes have been released.
what_they_can_do: Manage all corporate operations
Appoint and remove the Executive Director
Authorize officers/agents to enter contracts
Set member dues amounts
Create committees and define their terms of reference
Cast tie-breaking votes on committee decisions
Set strategic direction for the organization
what_they_cannot_do: Directly control on-chain treasury withdrawals (DReps vote on-chain)
transparency_gaps: No public board meeting minutes — ever
ED hiring process not disclosed publicly
No documented cases where board decisions were challenged by members

seats.note: Member-elected majority achieved Sep 2025. For the first 2 years (Jul 2023 - Oct 2025), appointed representatives from IOG and EMURGO held majority control.

members.name: Gerard Moroney
members.election_detail: IOG/IOHK COO — 3-year term as seed funder consideration

members.name: Nikhil Joshi
members.election_detail: EMURGO COO — 3-year term as seed funder consideration

members.name: Steven Lupien
members.election_detail: UW Center for Blockchain — academic seat

children.name: Executive Director
children.subtitle: Appointed by Board — Manages Day-to-Day
children.details: The ED supervises all business and affairs of the corporation. Appointed by the board — members have no vote on this appointment. Jack Briggs served as Interim ED from ~Oct 2025 and was permanently appointed Jan 2026 by unanimous board decision.
what_they_can_do: Supervise all business and affairs
Hire and manage staff (31 employees)
Coordinate between board, committees, and vendors
Set meeting agendas and control information flow
Manage vendor relationships and contract execution
transparency_gaps: No documented member input on the appointment

children.name: Intersect Staff
children.subtitle: Hired by ED/Board — No Member Input
children.details: 31 employees per the team page. Hired by the ED and board — members have no input on hiring. Staff control day-to-day operations, meeting agendas, and information flow. Only ~12 of 31 employees are publicly named.
transparency_gaps: No salaries disclosed for any staff member
No published org chart below leadership level

children.name: Standing Committees
children.subtitle: Advisory Only — Cannot Make Binding Decisions
children.details: 7 standing committees with members elected biannually (50% seats rotate per cycle). Per Intersect's own documentation, committees 'provide specialized guidance and advice to governance decision-makers.' They operate under 'terms of reference approved by the Board of Directors.' They are explicitly advisory — not governing bodies.
what_they_can_do: Provide recommendations to the board and community
Review and assess proposals (e.g., budget proposals)
Facilitate community discussions
Establish working groups for specific tasks
Vote internally on recommendations to bring forward
Provide subject matter expertise
what_they_cannot_do: Make binding decisions on treasury spending
Make any legally binding decision for the corporation
children.key_fact: The board can create committees, define their terms of reference, and cast tie-breaking votes on committee decisions. Committees exist at the pleasure of the board.

children.name: Technical Steering Committee (TSC)
children.details: Technical governance, parameter updates, incident response.

children.name: Open Source Committee (OSC)
children.details: Open-source strategy, bug bounties, security programs.

children.name: Budget Committee (CBC)
children.details: Budget guidance, audits, financial oversight. Advisory only — cannot approve spending.

children.name: Members
children.subtitle: Can Elect — Cannot Directly Decide
children.details: As of Feb 2025: 4,545 total members (2,712 associates, 1,781 individual, 49 enterprise). Only Individual ($10/year) and above have full voting rights. Members elect committee seats and 4 of 7 board seats. Members cannot directly approve budgets, contracts, or operational decisions. Election turnout has been low: 19.4% for committee elections (Oct 2025), 22% for board elections (Sep 2025).
what_they_can_do: Elect committee members (biannual elections)
Elect 4 of 7 board seats
Participate in working groups
Attend town halls and community events
Submit data and proposals
what_they_cannot_do: Hire or fire the Executive Director
Make binding decisions on contracts or operations
Elect 3 of 7 board seats (appointed by founding entities)
children.turnout_concern: Only ~20% of eligible members vote. This means the people making advisory recommendations through committees were elected by roughly 300 out of 1,500+ eligible voters.

children.name: Intersect Steering Committee (ISC)
children.subtitle: Internal Coordination — Mixed Selection
children.details: Composed of committee chairs (ex-officio) + 2 directly elected seats + admin leads (appointed). Coordinates across all committees. Not directly elected by members as a body.

key_facts: Committees are advisory. Per Intersect's own documentation, they 'provide specialized guidance and advice' — they do not govern.
The board manages all corporate affairs. Committees operate under 'terms of reference approved by the Board of Directors.'
The board appointed the ED (Jack Briggs) without member vote or public process.
For the first 2 years (Jul 2023 - Oct 2025), appointed seed-funder representatives held majority board control.
No board meeting minutes have ever been made public.
Only ~20% of eligible members participate in elections.
No salary or compensation data has been disclosed for any role.
19 of 31 staff members are not publicly named.
